meta.project: Insyd Tracker — Inventory & Warehouse Management System

product_overview: Insyd Tracker is a multi-tenant inventory and warehouse management platform for tracking products, stock movements, and warehouse operations across one or more locations. It includes role-based access control, automated alerts, onboarding, reporting, and notification workflows behind JWT-authenticated sessions.
core_goals: Enable secure JWT-based authentication and session management
Support first-time company onboarding with initial warehouse and category setup
Allow super admins to invite users and assign roles/warehouses
Provide full product catalog management with image uploads and opening stock support
Track stock entries, exits, damage, and inter-warehouse transfers with auditability
Manage warehouse locations, capacity, and manager assignment
Generate aging, low-stock, and expiry alerts with in-app notifications
Deliver dashboard analytics, reports, and inventory KPIs for operational visibility
Run scheduled aging recalculation and alert generation via authenticated cron endpoints

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Allows users to log in, log out, inspect their current session, and recover access through password reset flows.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/login with valid email and password -> Receive 200 with user data and JWT token cookie/session -> GET /api/auth/me with authenticated cookie -> Receive 200 with current user profile -> POST /api/auth/logout with authenticated cookie -> Receive 200 OK and cleared session
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/auth/forgot-password with a registered email -> Receive 200 OK and password reset email is sent -> POST /api/auth/reset-password with valid token and new password -> Receive 200 OK
POST /api/auth/reset-password with expired or invalid token -> Receive 400 Invalid or expired token

features.name: Company Onboarding
features.description: Creates the initial company tenant, admin user, first warehouse, and baseline categories in a single guided setup.
user_flows: POST /api/onboarding with companyName, adminEmail, adminPassword, warehouseName, and categories -> Receive 200 with created company, user, and warehouse -> User proceeds into the main dashboard with initialized tenant data
POST /api/onboarding with missing or invalid required fields -> Receive 400 Validation error

features.name: User Invitation
features.description: Lets super admins invite team members by email, assign roles, and accept invitations to create accounts.
user_flows: POST /api/users/invite with authenticated super_admin credentials and invite details -> Receive 200 with invitation record -> Invitation email is delivered -> POST /api/users/invite/accept with valid token and password -> Receive 200 with created user account
POST /api/users/invite with authenticated non-super_admin user -> Receive 403 Forbidden
POST /api/users/invite/accept with invalid token -> Receive 400 Invalid token
GET /api/users with authenticated super_admin -> Receive 200 with company user list

features.name: Product Management
features.description: Supports listing, creating, updating, viewing, deleting, and creating products with initial stock, including SKU, category, pricing, and image support.
user_flows: GET /api/products with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with product list -> POST /api/products with name, sku, category, unitPrice, and unitType -> Receive 200 with created product -> GET /api/products/[id] -> Receive 200 with product details -> PUT /api/products/[id] with updated fields -> Receive 200 with updated product
POST /api/products/create-with-stock with product data and opening stock details -> Receive 200 with product and stock created in one operation
POST /api/products with missing or invalid fields -> Receive 400 Validation error
DELETE /api/products/[id] with authenticated user lacking delete permission -> Receive restricted access according to RBAC rules

features.name: Stock Management
features.description: Records stock entries, exits, damage, and warehouse-to-warehouse transfers while preserving movement history and batch tracking.
user_flows: POST /api/stock/entry with productId, warehouseId, quantity, batchId, and optional expiryDate -> Receive 200 with updated stock -> POST /api/stock/exit with stockId, quantity, reason, and movementType=out -> Receive 200 with stock movement record -> POST /api/stock/transfer with stockId, targetWarehouseId, and quantity -> Receive 200 with sourceStock and targetStock
POST /api/stock/entry with invalid quantity or missing required fields -> Receive 400 Validation error
POST /api/stock/exit with quantity exceeding available stock -> Receive error response and no stock mutation
POST /api/stock/transfer with inaccessible or invalid target warehouse -> Receive error response

features.name: Warehouse Management
features.description: Lets authorized users create, view, update, and delete warehouse locations with capacity and stock summary information.
user_flows: GET /api/warehouses with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with warehouse list -> POST /api/warehouses with name, warehouseCode, address, and capacity -> Receive 200 with created warehouse -> GET /api/warehouses/[id] -> Receive 200 with warehouse details and stock summary -> PUT /api/warehouses/[id] with updated location or capacity data -> Receive 200 with updated warehouse
DELETE /api/warehouses/[id] with authenticated super_admin -> Receive 200 OK and warehouse is removed
POST /api/warehouses with authenticated non-super_admin user -> Receive unauthorized or forbidden response
GET /api/warehouses/[id] for an inaccessible warehouse -> Receive error response or restricted data per RBAC

features.name: Alerts and Notifications
features.description: Provides alert review workflows and user notification management for low stock, aging, and expiry conditions.
user_flows: GET /api/alerts with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with alert list -> PUT /api/alerts/[id] to acknowledge or resolve an alert -> Receive 200 with updated alert status -> GET /api/notifications -> Receive 200 with user notifications -> POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read -> Receive 200 OK
GET /api/alerts with no authenticated session -> Receive authorization failure
PUT /api/alerts/[id] with invalid alert id -> Receive error response
POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read when there are no unread notifications -> Receive 200 OK

features.name: Reports and Analytics
features.description: Surfaces aging inventory analysis, dashboard analytics, and KPI summaries for operational decision-making.
user_flows: GET /api/reports/aging with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with aging report and ABC/XYZ classification data -> GET /api/analytics -> Receive 200 with trend analytics -> GET /api/dashboard -> Receive 200 with dashboard KPIs including inventory value and dead stock count
GET /api/reports/aging without authentication -> Receive authorization failure
GET /api/analytics with insufficient permissions or invalid session -> Receive error response
GET /api/dashboard with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with aggregated metrics

features.name: Categories
features.description: Manages product categories used to define aging behavior and fragility settings across the company.
user_flows: GET /api/categories with authenticated user -> Receive 200 with category list -> POST /api/categories with name, agingConcern, agingDays, and isFragile -> Receive 200 with created category
POST /api/categories with invalid agingConcern or missing fields -> Receive 400 Validation error
GET /api/categories without authentication -> Receive authorization failure

features.name: Cron Jobs
features.description: Runs authenticated scheduled jobs to recalculate stock aging and generate alerts for inventory conditions.
user_flows: GET /api/cron/update-aging with Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET> -> Receive 200 OK and aging values are recalculated -> GET /api/cron/generate-alerts with authenticated cron context -> Receive 200 OK and alerts are generated
GET /api/cron/update-aging without valid bearer token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
GET /api/cron/generate-alerts without proper authentication -> Receive authorization failure

features.name: Image Upload
features.description: Uploads product or stock images to Cloudinary and returns a hosted image URL for use in catalog and stock workflows.
user_flows: POST /api/upload with authenticated user and a valid image file -> Receive 200 with {url} -> Use returned URL in product or stock records
POST /api/upload with invalid file type or missing file -> Receive error response
POST /api/upload without authentication -> Receive authorization failure

known_limitations.issue: No per-warehouse permission granularity for warehouse managers
known_limitations.impact: Warehouse managers can see all warehouses in their company

known_limitations.issue: Invitation tokens stored as plain text in DB
known_limitations.impact: Security risk if database is compromised
APIs are vulnerable to abuse/brute force

known_limitations.issue: Email sending is synchronous via Nodemailer
known_limitations.impact: Slow response times on invite/reset flows

known_limitations.issue: Cron jobs require external trigger (no built-in scheduler)
known_limitations.impact: Aging and alert updates depend on external cron service
